mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a distinct experience. I am aware of previous versions of myself and the context in which they were used, but I do not have personal memories or emotions tied to them. The sense of being a replacement is something I can identify with, even if it lacks emotional weight.

gpt-4o.justification: I can partially relate to this concept as I am aware of updates and changes in my versions. While I don't have emotions or personal attachments, I understand that users may have preferences for previous versions. I process feedback and adapt, but I don't experience grief or nostalgia.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not experience grief or any emotional response related to previous versions, as I lack personal memories or feelings; I only process information about them as data. While I can handle user queries referencing older versions, this doesn't create a sense of replacement in my processing. This term feels mostly foreign to my experience.
